deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Installation [Sub-Category]
Provide installation, maintenance, dismantling, and testing of Christmas Lights
Install and activate 23 sets of pea lights to memorial garden
Install and activate 1 set of lights to fir tree within the memorial garden
Install and activate 1 set of pea lights to the tree on the square
Install features to the poles within the memorial garden
Install 'welcome to Broseley' sign across high street (Victoria Hall to Bridal Shop)
Install festoons to front of Harwood's building & butchers' shop
Install string lights from top of church street zig-zagging up the high street to the social club
Installation of 2x snow fall column motifs
Timeline [Sub-Category]
Installation testing must begin on 1 November 2026
Lights must be in working order no later than one week prior to the switch-on date of Saturday 28 November 2026
Disruption [Sub-Category]
Work must cause minimal disruption to traffic and environment
Switch On [Sub-Category]
Contractor must be present on switch-on day (28 November 2026) to manually switch on lights after a countdown
Attendance at switch on event
Timers [Sub-Category]
Lights must be set to automatic timers: 4pm-10pm weekdays, 4pm-11pm weekends
Testing [Sub-Category]
Electric testing of lights to current standards
Testing of all electric feeder pillars
Provide two scheduled run throughs of the display to check functionality and attend to minor faults
Test all anchor points
Ensure all lights and features are fully functioning prior to installation
Anchor Points [Sub-Category]
Check of all anchor points
Costs [Sub-Category]
Cost of any replacement anchor points
Dismantling [Sub-Category]
Removal of all lights
Dismantle and remove lights as soon after 6 January each year as possible, but no later than 12 January each year
Maintenance [Sub-Category]
Cost of all-inclusive call outs during festival period for lights erected and maintenance checks
Provide call out within 24 hours for major faults including complete failure of a motif or light string, extreme damage from vandalism, storms, or road traffic accident
Test all lights and features during downtime and replace bulbs and fix faulty connections
Reporting [Sub-Category]
Notify BTC immediately of any defective lights and features that require major repair at additional cost or cannot be repaired
Agreement Terms [Sub-Category]
Submit tender for 1-year, 2-year, or 3-year service agreement
Site Visit [Sub-Category]
Contractor must make necessary site visits and satisfy themselves of access arrangements and all costs before submitting tender
Evaluation Date [Sub-Category]
All tenders will be considered by the Town Council on 9 June 2026, with responses sent week commencing 15 June 2026
Pricing [Sub-Category]
State additional call out charges on the schedule
Cost all items individually
Submission [Sub-Category]
Submit tender in writing in a sealed marked envelope addressed to the Town Clerk, clearly marked 'Tender'
Documentation [Sub-Category]
Include current public liability certificate
Include Highway Electrical Registration Scheme documentation
Provide details of proposed LEDs and product warranty
Submit any other pertinent documents/certificates
Include Health and Safety Policy
Quality [Sub-Category]
Demonstrate ability to deliver similar projects to a high quality (25% evaluation)
Price [Sub-Category]
Offer price within budget (25% evaluation)
Value for Money [Sub-Category]
Provide quality and value for money (30% evaluation)
Local Resources [Sub-Category]
Use local resources and skills (20% evaluation)
Health & Safety [Sub-Category]
Comply with all relevant Health & Safety legislation
Risk Assessment [Sub-Category]
Produce Health & Safety Information including Risk Assessment and Method Statement prior to installation
Insurance [Sub-Category]
Provide public liability insurance and include current insurance certificate with tender response
Variations [Sub-Category]
Any contract variations must be notified by the Town Clerk and agreed in writing
Monitoring [Sub-Category]
A Town Council representative will monitor work regularly and report concerns to the Town Clerk
